  "findings": [
    "The tension in each sling leg is 8,000 lb, which is below the tagged vertical WLL of 9,500 lb."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Proceed with the planned lift using the specified rigging configuration."
  ],
  "rationale": "For a symmetric two-leg sling arrangement, the tension in each leg is calculated as (Load / 2) / sin(angle). Here, (8,000 lb / 2) / sin(30 degrees) = 4,000 / 0.5 = 8,000 lb. Since the tension of 8,000 lb is less than the rated vertical Working Load Limit (WLL) of 9,500 lb per leg, the configuration is safe and compliant.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.9",
    "OSHA 1926.251"
  ]
}
